---
title: "CodeBook Lab"
date: "2026"
date-format: "YYYY"
abstract: "A Python package for computational social science that benchmarks local LLM annotation runs against human labels across model choice, prompt style, example use, chat mode, reasoning mode, and sampling settings."
hide-abstract: true
packagelink: "https://pypi.org/project/codebook-lab/"
docslink: "/codebook-lab/"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    template-partials:
      - "../partials/title-block.html"
---

CodeBook Lab is a Python package for running validation-first LLM annotation experiments against human-coded benchmark data. It takes a `codebook.json` and labelled `ground-truth.csv` exported from [CodeBook Studio](codebook-studio.qmd), removes human labels before annotation, and scores model outputs against the held-out labels.

Researchers can evaluate local Ollama models systematically across the dimensions that matter for text-as-data research:

- **Model choice and model size**: compare any locally available Ollama models
- **Prompt style and example use**: compare standard, persona, and chain-of-thought wrappers with or without codebook examples
- **Chat and reasoning modes**: test fresh chats per query, per-text chats, continuous chats, and model reasoning settings
- **Sampling settings**: sweep over temperature, top-p, and related generation parameters

Experiments are controlled from Python, so users can start with a single run, scale up to multiple experiment specifications, or iterate through larger parameter grids when needed. Because the codebook and labelled data stay constant across runs, each configuration can be compared against the same human labels.

```{=html}
<style>
.codebook-flow {
  --codebook-border: #7a5a65;
  --codebook-card-bg: #f7f1ec;
  --codebook-data-bg: #fcf8f5;
  --codebook-title: #5f3b4a;
  --codebook-text: #4a3a40;
  --codebook-code-bg: rgba(95, 59, 74, 0.08);
  --codebook-code-border: rgba(122, 90, 101, 0.22);
  --codebook-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(220px, 1fr) 80px minmax(220px, 1fr);
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: center;
  margin: 1.5rem 0 1rem 0;
}

.quarto-dark .codebook-flow {
  --codebook-border: #7b9bb5;
  --codebook-card-bg: #253544;
  --codebook-data-bg: #1f2d3a;
  --codebook-title: #b7d0e4;
  --codebook-text: #d7e6f3;
  --codebook-code-bg: #13212d;
  --codebook-code-border: rgba(183, 208, 228, 0.18);
  --codebook-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.22);
}

.codebook-card {
  border: 2px solid var(--codebook-border);
  border-radius: 12px;
  background: var(--codebook-card-bg);
  color: var(--codebook-text);
  padding: 1rem 1.1rem;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px var(--codebook-shadow);
}

.codebook-card h3 {
  margin: 0 0 0.6rem 0;
  font-size: 1.15rem;
  color: var(--codebook-title);
}

.codebook-card ul {
  margin: 0;
  padding-left: 1.1rem;
}

.codebook-card li {
  margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
  color: var(--codebook-text);
}

.codebook-arrow {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  color: var(--codebook-title);
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.codebook-bridge {
  grid-column: 1 / -1;
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 80px 1fr;
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: center;
  margin-top: -0.25rem;
}

.codebook-data {
  border: 1.5px dashed var(--codebook-border);
  border-radius: 10px;
  background: var(--codebook-data-bg);
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  color: var(--codebook-text);
}

.codebook-data strong {
  color: var(--codebook-title);
}

.codebook-card code,
.codebook-card li code,
.codebook-data code {
  color: var(--codebook-text);
  background: var(--codebook-code-bg);
  border: 1px solid var(--codebook-code-border);
  border-radius: 0.45rem;
  padding: 0.08rem 0.35rem;
}

.quarto-dark .codebook-card code,
.quarto-dark .codebook-card li code,
.quarto-dark .codebook-data code {
  color: var(--codebook-text) !important;
  background: var(--codebook-code-bg) !important;
  border-color: var(--codebook-code-border) !important;
}

@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .codebook-flow,
  .codebook-bridge {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }

  .codebook-arrow {
    transform: rotate(90deg);
  }
}
</style>

<div class="codebook-flow">
  <div class="codebook-card">
    <h3>CodeBook Studio</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Define the annotation task</li>
      <li>Annotate texts with humans</li>
      <li>Export <code>codebook.json</code> and <code>ground-truth.csv</code></li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="codebook-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

  <div class="codebook-card">
    <h3>CodeBook Lab</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Run LLM annotation experiments</li>
      <li>Isolate model, prompt, chat, reasoning, and sampling effects</li>
      <li>Score outputs against human labels</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="codebook-bridge">
    <div class="codebook-data">
      <strong>Shared inputs</strong><br>
      <code>codebook.json</code> + human-annotated <code>ground-truth.csv</code>
    </div>

    <div class="codebook-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

    <div class="codebook-data">
      <strong>Experiment outputs</strong><br>
      Accuracy, agreement, runtime, energy, and emissions metrics
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

CodeBook Lab also supports human-reliability workflows: it can validate coder CSVs, calculate inter-coder agreement, identify unresolved disagreements, and build an adjudication queue that can be completed in CodeBook Studio. Final outputs include run-level annotation quality, runtime, prompt and response length, energy consumption, and estimated carbon emissions, with aggregate metrics logged for cross-experiment comparison.

The package ships with a bundled starter task so you can test the workflow before plugging in your own data. For installation, experiments, human reliability, and the full API, see the [CodeBook Lab documentation](../codebook-lab/). Citation details for both CodeBook Lab and CodeBook Studio are available on the [CodeBook citation page](../codebook-lab/citation.html).

Full package documentation is available at [lorcanmclaren.com/codebook-lab](/codebook-lab/).
